response.justification: Maria is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling interests — she built test runners, assertion libraries, and worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments — but the role's core requirements are strong Java/Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience, none of which are substantively evidenced. Her career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, with only incidental Java exposure. The IDE-internals interest is real but insufficient to offset the missing JVM foundation for an enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling role.
strengths: Hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Long track record across low-latency systems, complex codebases, and diverse stacks, with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Active open-source maintainer and community educator — strong communication and knowledge-sharing signals
concerns: No substantive Java or Kotlin experience; Java appears only briefly and Kotlin not at all
No evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework work
Deep JVM understanding — a hard requirement — is not demonstrated anywhere in the resume

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling background
